claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 10 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

Let me start by gathering some information about your situation. Child Benefit eligibility is assessed for each child individually, so I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who is 10 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 10 years old.

Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, **does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to understand the situation a bit better.

**Who does Alex currently live with?** (For example, the other parent, another relative, or someone else?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. When a child lives with someone else, there are specific rules about who can claim Child Benefit.

Let me ask: **Do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep?** (For example, do you provide regular financial support, pay for clothing, food, or other costs for Alex?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out with some things for Alex - I contribute towards their costs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about the living arrangement.

**Is the other parent (who Alex lives with) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me also ask: **Do you contribute at least the same amount (or more) towards Alex's upkeep as the amount of Child Benefit that would be payable?**

To give you context, the current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't contribute that much. I help out with some costs each week, but it's less than the Child Benefit amount.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**. Here's why:

**Key factors in your situation:**
- Alex does not live with you - they live with their other parent
- The other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- While you do contribute towards Alex's costs, you contribute less than the Child Benefit amount (£25.60 per week)

**The rules state that:**
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any given time
- When a child doesn't live with you, you can only claim Child Benefit if you contribute at least as much as the Child Benefit amount towards the child's upkeep
- If someone else is already claiming and living with the child, and you don't meet the contribution threshold, you cannot make a claim

In your case, since Alex's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit and Alex lives with them, and your financial contribution is below the Child Benefit threshold, the other parent is the appropriate person to receive the benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if the other parent stops claiming), you may want to reconsider your eligibility at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's clear. Thank you for explaining that to me. I understand now that I'm not eligible because Alex lives with their other parent who's already claiming, and I don't contribute enough to meet the threshold. I appreciate your help.
